exemplar.title: To what extent On Running unique selling proposition strategies changed the premium athletic footwear market, resulting in a shift in the financial performance of the company?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent On Running unique selling proposition strategies changed the premium athletic footwear market, resulting in a shift in the financial performance of the company?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Title communicates the investigationâs focus effectively, establishing clear context
Introduction provides detailed background on On Running and its USP
âImpact of CloudTecâ section is logically organized and follows from technical explanation to market implications
weaknesses: Research question combines two aims and is somewhat broad, causing occasional descriptive detours
Methodology section lacks critical justification of source selection and discussion of reliability
Occasional sentence fragments and abrupt endings reduce overall clarity and flow
Structural coherence issues (e.g., misplaced summary of limitations) detract from focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates the topic accurately and provides sufficient context, but the focus drifts at times due to a broad research question and the methodology lacks critical depth. Overall performance sits in the middle of the 3â6 band, warranting 4 marks.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of business terminology (e.g., USP, P/E ratio, segmentation)
Well-integrated revenue comparison table enhances understanding of competitor trends
Clear subject knowledge linking marketing and financial theory to the On Running case
weaknesses: Application of source material is uneven, with long quotations often unanalysed
Literature review lacks thematic synthesis and critical evaluation of sources
Minor inaccuracies and oversimplifications in linking concepts to case (e.g., price sensitivity vs income level)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Terminology and conceptual knowledge are solid, but the application and critique of source material lack consistency and depth. These features align with a mid-band performance, justifying 3 marks.

strengths: Good variety of reputable secondary sources clearly relevant to the research question
Analytical treatment is strongâaccurate ratio calculations and effective benchmarking
Insightful discussion on premium pricing effects and contextualised gross margin analysis
weaknesses: No primary research and limited engagement with source bias or reliability
Some causal claims (e.g., revenue growth solely due to USP) are asserted without sufficient triangulation
Evaluation remains brief with few systematic recommendations or alternative perspectives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and analysis are consistently good, with clear relevance and well-supported conclusions. Evaluation and argument depth, while competent, lack the sustained critical rigour for the top band. A mark of 9 reflects strong but not outstanding critical thinking.

strengths: All required structural components are present and logically ordered
Table of contents and section headings guide the reader effectively
weaknesses: Inconsistent subheading indentation and occasional abrupt page breaks disrupt visual flow
Minor citation and formatting lapses (truncated references, inconsistent in-text styles)
Several sentences end mid-thought, affecting readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and formatting meet requirements overall, but the presence of multiple minor layout and typographical issues limits the performance to a solid mid-band, meriting 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is stated verbatim on the title page and revisited throughout the report, giving the essay a recognizable focus. However, its double aim (market change and financial performance) remains somewhat broad, leading to occasional descriptive detours and overlap between chapters. Connection between some sub-sections (e.g., Kotler analysis, benchmarking table) and the core question is present but not always tight. The focus is therefore sound yet not consistently sharp, meriting the upper level of the 3â4 band.

The discussion follows a logical path from technology to market effects to finance and ends with stated limitations, showing some evaluative intent. Yet evaluations are often brief and rely on general assertions rather than rigorous weighing of evidence or alternative perspectives. Recommendations are implicit rather than systematically argued. This aligns with a competent but not highly critical discussion, meriting the lower end of the 4â6 band.

The student demonstrates clear understanding of business concepts such as USP, segmentation, Kotlerâs principles, profitability ratios and benchmarking. Explanations link theory to the On Running case in a coherent manner, and technical financial metrics are calculated correctly. Minor inaccuracies (e.g., conflation of price sensitivity with income level) and occasional oversimplifications prevent a top-band award, but overall knowledge remains sound, justifying the higher mark within the 3â4 band.

Business terminology (gross margin, P/E ratio, USP, CloudTec, segmentation, CAGR) is used accurately and consistently, and key marketing/financial concepts are applied appropriately. A handful of grammatical slips and a few imprecise phrases (e.g., âcreating an impactâ) marginally affect precision, but not to a significant extent. Overall usage fits the top of the 3â4 band.

The student offers clear analytical treatment: ratio calculations are accurately performed, financial data are compared across firms, and links are drawn between CloudTecâs attributes and market/financial outcomes. However, several causal claims (e.g., revenue growth solely due to USP) are asserted with limited triangulation; counter-arguments and macro-context are touched on only briefly. The depth is therefore good but not fully sophisticated, warranting a mid-band score of 5.

Most cited tables, graphs and quotations are relevant and help illustrate market position, technology or finance. The student integrates revenue charts, ratio tables and academic findings into the text, but some usages remain descriptive (e.g., long quotations or figures followed by minimal commentary). Limited critique of source limitations further reduces effectiveness. These features align with the mid-band (3â4) for application of source material; a lower-band score of 3 reflects the uneven depth of use.

The essay draws on a reasonable range of secondary sourcesâacademic articles on biomechanics and consumer behaviour, company financial statements, industry reports and media piecesâshowing some informed selection. Nonetheless, there is no primary research, and the methodology section offers only a brief, largely descriptive outline without critically justifying source reliability or data-collection choices. This places the work solidly but not strongly in the 3â4 band; a mid-band score is appropriate.

The essay draws on a good variety of current, reputable secondary sourcesâpeer-reviewed biomechanics studies, industry statistics, company filings and media analysesâthat are clearly pertinent to the research question. While breadth and currency are evident, absence of primary data and limited engagement with source bias mean the research is solid rather than outstanding. These characteristics place it in the 4â6 band; a score of 5 recognises its above-average strength without suggesting the consistency needed for the 7â9 band.

Font, spacing and table/figure captions are generally consistent; citations are mostly complete, and the reference list follows a recognisable style. Nevertheless, small lapses persist: line-wrapping of URLs, a few truncated citations, and inconsistent in-text referencing formats (e.g., missing years in some quotes). These minor flaws place layout and formatting comfortably in the 3â4 band but warrant a mid-band score of 3 rather than the maximum.

The student states the topic explicitly in the title, provides clear background on On Runningâs origins, and explains why CloudTec is considered a distinctive USP. Contextual details about the premium athletic-footwear market and leading competitors help readers understand the investigationâs relevance. Occasional language slips (e.g., sentence fragments) and some repetitive phrasing reduce absolute clarity, but overall communication is accurate and reasonably comprehensive, justifying a score in the upper half of the 5â6 band.

All required structural componentsâtitle page with word count, abstract, contents, numbered pages, chapters, references, tables/figuresâare present and logically ordered. Headings guide the reader and financial data are sensibly grouped. Minor issues (occasional abrupt page breaks, inconsistent sub-heading indentation) do not impair overall appropriateness, justifying the top mark in the 3â4 band.

annotations.comment: The title section communicates the investigationâs focus accurately and effectively, establishing clear context for the analysis of On Runningâs USP and its market impact.
The research question is clearly stated but combines two distinct aims (market change and financial impact), making it somewhat broad; consider narrowing to sharpen focus.
The student provides a word count at the start of the report, fulfilling the formatting requirement with transparency.
The table of contents presents all required sections and page numbers in a logical, easy-to-navigate format, supporting clear report structure.
Indentation of sub-sections varies (e.g., extra spaces before 'Literature review'), creating inconsistency in visual hierarchy; align indent levels for clarity.
The introduction effectively communicates On Runningâs origin and the significance of its USP, establishing clear background and relevance.
Several sentences are fragments (e.g., starting with 'Which uniquenessâ¦'); revise for grammatical completeness to enhance clarity and flow.
The 'Impact of Cloud Tec' section follows logically from technical explanation to market implications, demonstrating effective organisation.
The methodology description lists source types but offers no critical rationale for selection or discussion of reliability; expand to justify methodological choices.
The literature review enumerates studies but lacks thematic synthesis or critique of their limitations; organise sources by theme and evaluate their relevance.
Clear section heading for Market Segmentation enhances navigational flow and demonstrates appropriate structure.
The quotation from The Times is uncontextualised; assess its credibility and discuss potential bias or limitations for a more critical evaluation.
Application of Kotlerâs principles shows clear understanding of marketing theory and its relevance to On Runningâs segmentation strategy.
A substantial Nike quotation is provided with little follow-up evaluation; critique how this example supports or limits your comparative argument.
Figure 3âs detailed revenue comparison table is well-integrated and enhances understanding of competitor financial trends.
Discussion of Figure 3 overlooks external factors (e.g., currency fluctuations, market shocks) that may have influenced revenue trends; consider these in analysis.
Footnote superscript formatting is inconsistent; unify footnote style and ensure numbering matches standard conventions.
The Adidas comparison concisely links product technology to marketing strategy, reflecting clear subject knowledge and coherence.
Benchmarking table presents multiple quantitative metrics effectively, demonstrating sound analytical approach to competitor comparison.
Commentary on the first criterion (ânumber of shoesâ) cuts off mid-idea (âsince more options areâ); complete the evaluation for clarity.
Analysis of social media engagement uses an average but doesnât explain its calculation; provide methodology or data source for the average follower count.
Figure 2âs source is cited but lacks commentary on its origin and reliability; evaluate the visual dataâs validity and any assumptions made.
The paragraph on Steven et al. (2021) ends mid-sentence ('...success in the athletic'), which disrupts readability; complete the discussion.
Chapter 2 heading is clearly labelled and consistent with earlier chapters, supporting a coherent report structure.
Insight into how premium pricing may affect purchasing decisions demonstrates strong critical thinking about consumer behaviour and brand positioning.
The summary of On Runningâs limitations appears in the analysis section rather than a dedicated reflection or conclusion; consider relocating for structural coherence.
Table 2 organizes key financial metrics (e.g., gross margin, P/E) in a clear, comparable format, enhancing the robustness of the financial analysis.
The final sentence ends abruptly ('...to produce a better reception'); complete the thought to maintain coherence.
Long technical quotation from MÃ¼ller (2024) is followed by minimal analysis; integrate critical commentary to link it back to your argument.
In-depth discussion of gross profit margin contextualised against industry averages demonstrates sophisticated analytical treatment.
